sections.title: Talking to Ranulph after starting the quest before finding out why he's distracted

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. If you don't mind me saying, you seem quite preoccupied.

sections.title: Talking to Ranulph after watching the ent interact with  the totem

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Did you see what that ent did?

sections.title: Talking to Ranulph or Isadora before fixing the totem

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Any luck getting that totem rebuilt?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: Hurry up! You don't want the ents to stay sad, do you?

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: I reckon you'll just need one set of logs. Oak logs should be enough, but I do wonder if using better wood would help it survive the elements better...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Oh, sorry! I was lost in thought. You gave me quite the start!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does something have you preoccupied?

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Yes! It's these ents you see! I'd never seen anything like them until I came here. Look, here comes one now...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... It's like some sort of ritual.

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Exactly! These totems are dotted all around here. They seem to be very important to the ents, but I don't know why.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you asked around?

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Yes, but the locals didn't really give me much to go on. All they told me is that the ents, totems and spirits are all part of the natural cycle here in the valley.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Well, why don't we take a closer look at this totem?

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Good idea. If nothing else, it will be useful to document the carvings on it. Come, let's have a look...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a beautiful totem.

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Indeed! Look at this bit!

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: Uh oh! The funny man broke the totem!

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Oh... There's a child.

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: Excuse me? I have a name! I'm Isadora, and you're the funny man who gets confused by the ents! I've been watching you.

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Well, actually my name is Ranulph, not funny man...

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: Funny man Ranulph broke the totem! Ents won't be happy with you!

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Hmm... I think the child's assessment is correct. The ents probably won't be happy about what we've done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure 'we' is the right word...

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: It's okay, funny man. The totems always end up breaking. The ents get sad for a while, but if you fix them, they end up all happy again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So we need to fix the totem. How?

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Let's see... First, we'll need some lumber to rebuild the pole. I reckon one set of logs will do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any specific type?

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: It looks like it was made from oak, so presumably oak logs should be enough. However, I do wonder if using better wood would help it survive the elements better...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Or survive interference from... you know... us.

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Hmm... The pole is only going to be the beginning though. We're going to need to carve it... I really should have made more notes on the carvings...

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: Notes? Notes are boring! I know how to carve them! Daddy showed me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I guess you can consult with Isadora on how to carve the totem. While you do that, I'll get to work on rebuilding the pole.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, sorry to have disturbed you then.

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Not a problem.

sections.title: Attempting to carve before learning how

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: That's a good start.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's next?

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: Now you need to carve the animals!

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Yes, according to Isadora, the carvings that were on the totem represented the animal spirits around it. However, the animals were disturbed when the totem fell. Some left, but new ones arrived.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Meaning?

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: Meaning you need to carve the right ones, silly.

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: The right ones being the animals that are currently in this area.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I supposed that makes sense. I'll give it a go.

sections.title: Talking to Ranulph or Isadora again before carving the animals

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: Have you carved the animals yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Don't forget, you'll need to make carvings of the animal spirits that are currently in this area.

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: But you won't be able to do it without a knife! Make some space and I'll give you one!

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: But you won't be able to do it without a knife! Here!

sections.title: If the player carves an animal not nearby

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: That's not right!

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Remember, you need to make carvings of the animal spirits that are currently in this area.

sections.title: Attempting to decorate before knowing how to

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: You've quite the hand at carving, my friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Now, the finishing touches. Apparently, the ents appreciate it when wooden items are returned to the forest.

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: Yes! You need to help them finish the cycle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cycle?

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: The cycle of life, death and decay, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: Funny man Ranulph is learning!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then. So wooden items? You mean things like bows and shields?

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Exactly! Try decorating the totem with those kinds of items. I reckon four should do. Oh, and be sure to use the same wood you used for the totem itself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back once I've done that.

sections.title: Talking to Ranulph or Isadora again before decorating the totem

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: How's the decorating of that totem going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: You need to use the wooden things. Bows! Shields!

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: What Isadora said basically. I reckon four should do. Oh, and be sure to use the same wood you used for the totem itself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There we go! I's say that's the totem finished!

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Look at that! I think it looks even better than it did before!

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: You did a good job! Now we have to see if you've made the ents happy again!

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: I couldn't have imagined a better outcome! Not only have the ents accepted our new totem, but they've also left something behind!

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: It's a gift! The ents are happy again! They're saying thank you for helping! Go on, take it!

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Well, this is definitely the most 'dialogue' I've had with the ents. Isadora here has been a great help as well!

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: Thanks, funny man Ranulph. You're not so bad yourself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now?

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: It seems the ents are very appreciative of those who maintain their totems. Given you seem to have a knack for it, you might want to keep helping them. They have plenty of other totem sites in the valley.

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: Yes! The ents like it when you help! They'll give you more gifts!

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: As for me, I'm going to keep studying them. I've learnt so much today, both from Isadora and from the ents themselves. I've already documented it all, but I'm sure there's more to discover!

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Speaking of which, here, take a notebook for yourself. I've left a copy of my notes in it, but you can also use it to document your own findings.

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Be sure to check in once in a while to compare findings. I'll happily trade items I find around the valley in exchange for your research assistance!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds great, thanks!

lines.speaker: Isadora
lines.text: Goddbye, other funny person. You did great!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, Isadora.

lines.speaker: Ranulph
lines.text: Until next time, friend.
